title: Alternative to pasting images in text or rich-text columns

posts.author: OmarConte (Omar Conte)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

I’m new here and I’m evaluating to move from ClickUp to Monday, but I noticed that it is not possible to paste images/screen shots into a text or rich-text field.

Only on Updates it is possible, but (in ClickUp) I normally paste screen shots in the task description in the middle of the text.

How can this be done in Monday? What are the alternatives and best practices?

Thanks

Omar

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Omar! Welcome to the monday Community! Yes, the Text and Long Text Columns only support text formatting at this time. I understand that you’d prefer to integrate the screenshots in your description, but perhaps you could utilize a Files Column or monday Docs?

Instead of utilizing a Text/Long Text Column, you can insert the task description into a monday Doc for your task/item. This way, you can still paste the screenshot in the middle of the description. You can add a monday Doc Column to your board to generate a monday Doc for each task specifically.

Alternatively, if you add a Files Column, if you’d like to annotate the screenshots, you can leave a comment for each screenshot with the task description! Both options I’ve shared will require you/your team to open the doc or screenshot to reference the description, though. Just sharing some ideas with you—let me know if you’d like to brainstorm other workarounds together.

posts.author: OmarConte (Omar Conte)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

thanks for your hints.

I will try to see if I can live with the Updates module.

Omar
